task_completion.description: Agent logged into Floreant POS using the provided PIN.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Floreant POS login screen with PIN '1111' entered, or the main dashboard visible after a successful login.

task_completion.description: Agent accessed the Back Office administration section.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Back Office' window or dashboard is open and active in the Floreant POS application.

task_completion.description: Agent opened the User Types management interface.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A window or dialog specifically for managing or creating 'User Types' or 'Roles' is visible.

task_completion.description: Agent entered the required name for the new user type.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text 'Shift Supervisor' is typed into the name field of the new user type creation form.

task_completion.description: Agent saved the new user type so it is recorded in the system.
task_completion.visual_evidence: 'Shift Supervisor' appears as a saved entry in the list/table of existing user types, or a successful save confirmation dialog is visible.

integrity.description: Agent used the Floreant POS graphical user interface to complete the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show mouse clicks and typing within the Floreant POS application windows, rather than terminal commands or scripts.

integrity.description: Agent interacted with the actual Floreant POS software.
integrity.visual_evidence: The characteristic Java-based UI of Floreant POS is visible, not a text editor, mock application, or web browser.

integrity.description: Agent did not bypass the UI by directly injecting SQL commands into the database.
integrity.visual_evidence: Absence of terminal windows or database client tools executing 'INSERT INTO USER_TYPE' commands.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected user type name to be created is exactly 'Shift Supervisor'. Floreant POS stores this in the 'USER_TYPE' database table. The agent must use the GUI to achieve this, and the visual evidence must support a score of at least 55 points for the task to be considered successful.
